commit.message:
style: 月次レポートKPIカードの指標名を初心者向け表現に変更

各カードタイトルを「わかりやすい日本語（専門用語）」形式に:
- ページビュー → 見られた回数（ページビュー）
- セッション → 訪問回数（セッション）
- ユーザー → 見に来た人の数（ユーザー）
- 新規ユーザー → はじめての人の数（新規ユーザー）
- リピーター → また来てくれた人（リピーター）
- 平均滞在時間 → しっかり見られた時間（平均滞在時間）
- ゴール数 → ゴール数（コンバージョン）

カッコ内の専門用語は .kpi-term で小さく薄く表示

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: grid view and studio broken by stale file: paths (#43)

* Fix: grid view empty slides and broken studio from stale file: paths

Two fixes:

1. Grid view thumbnails rendered empty because AnimationProvider was
   missing — Animated components defaulted to hidden (opacity: 0).
   Added AnimationProvider with showAllAnimations=true, matching how
   list view already works.

2. The create command hardcoded absolute file: paths to the current
   Conductor workspace directory into scaffolded package.json files.
   When workspaces rotate, bun install fails (ENOENT), node_modules/
   promptslide doesn't exist, Tailwind's @source finds nothing to
   scan, and the entire studio shell renders without CSS. Removed the
   file: rewriting — scaffolded projects now always use the npm
   registry version. Use bun link for local dev testing instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use CLI version dynamically for scaffolded promptslide dependency

Instead of hardcoding "^0.2.0", read the CLI's own version at scaffold
time so new projects always track the current minor version range.

commit.message:
fix: InfoBox toggle div i.p.v. button (voorkom button-in-button hydration error)

InlineEdit rendert een <button> voor het potlood-icoon. Wanneer InlineEdit
in de title-prop van InfoBox zit, ontstond een <button>-in-<button>-nesting
die door HTML verboden is en een Next.js hydration error veroorzaakte.
Vervangen door <div role="button"> met keyboard-handlers.

feat: tables d'apparition authentiques par zone + départ zone 1

TAUX D'APPARITION
- Chaque zone a sa propre table de rencontre avec Pokémon pondérés
- Zone 1 (Route 1) : Rattata 30%, Pidgey 27%, Chenipan 13%, Aspicot 13%,
  Abo 7%, Pikachu 5%, Mélofée 3%, Rondoudou 2%
- Zone 2 (Mt. Code) : Racaillou/Nosferapti communs, Ronflex rare 2%
- Zone 3 (Route Victoire) : Gen 2, Embrylex rare 5%
- Zone 4 (Forêt Obscure) : Gen 3, Terhal et Milobellus très rares
- Zone 5 (Île Écarlate) : Gen 4, Riolu et Motisma rares
- Zone 6 (Sanctuaire) : Gen 5, Reshiram 4% et Zekrom 3% légendaires !
- Niveaux adaptés par zone : Zone 1 = 2-12, Zone 6 = 45-65

DÉPART ZONE 1
- currentZone démarre toujours à 1 (au lieu de calculer depuis les repos)
- Le joueur progresse en changeant de zone manuellement

commit.message:
Fix npm install: use pipe for download, wait for file flush before extract

The manual file.write()/file.end() approach caused truncated downloads
because resolve() was called before the file was fully written to disk.
Switch back to res.pipe(file) with file.on("finish") callback. Also fix
progress bar formatting.

fix: resolve stale SIM assignments on modems (Issue #2)

- Migration 016: clear 6 stale SIMs on offline modem 865827078940772,
  clear 1 stale SIM on active modem 865827078942505, mark offline modem
  as disconnected
- Bug 1 fix (control.js): reconciliation now marks status IN
  ('active','connected','online') as pending, not just 'connected';
  daemon reports 'active' so absent modems were never reconciled
- Bug 2 fix (control.js): add SIM eviction after each sync — any SIM
  pointing to a reported modem but absent from the received list is
  set to current_modem_id=NULL, status='inactive'

commit.message:
fix: use SET LOCAL in transactions for duplicate queries

Heavy pg_trgm self-join queries on 35K+ recipes were hitting the
default 30s statement_timeout. Using SET LOCAL inside Prisma
interactive transactions ensures:
1. SET and query run on the same pooled connection
2. statement_timeout extended to 60s for the heavy query
3. Fallback query also gets extended timeout (45s)
4. Fallback has its own try/catch so failures don't crash

cookbook: add x402scan MCP tools example (#6555)

# Add x402scan MCP Tools Example

![Agent with
money](https://f.agentupload.dev/fdawr3pwc5/edited_photo.png)

Agents that pay for APIs autonomously using USDC on Base. Access 100+
paid data sources: enrichment, web scraping, maps, social media,
image/video generation.

## Examples

**Basic (1-3):** Onboarding, research agent, multi-agent team

**Advanced (4-8):** 
- Tool hooks for real-time cost tracking
- Structured outputs with cost attribution
- Skills integration
- Learning Machine (agent optimizes spending over time)
- Production controls with hard budget limits

## Setup

```bash
npx @x402scan/mcp install
```

First run auto-generates wallet. Fund with USDC on Base.

**Available APIs:** enrichx402.com and stablestudio.io
- Apollo/Clado (person/company): $0.02-0.20
- Firecrawl/Exa (web scraping): $0.01-0.05
- Google Maps: $0.02-0.08
- Grok (Twitter), Reddit: $0.02
- Serper (news/shopping): $0.04

## Agno Features

- **Tool hooks:** Pre/post execution tracking
- **Structured outputs:** Type-safe results with cost per source
- **Learning Machine:** Agent learns API performance, stores memories in
JsonDb
- **Teams:** Shared wallet with per-member tracking

## Testing

Tested with live wallet - wallet ops, Apollo enrichment, tool hooks,
JsonDb storage all verified.

## Links

- [x402scan-mcp](https://x402scan.com/mcp)
- [x402scan-skills](https://github.com/merit-systems/x402scan-skills) 
- [x402 protocol](https://x402.org)

Sent by @sragss

commit.message:
docs: remove transition banner and update scope descriptions (#551)

* refactor: rename CAPZ_USER to CAPI_USER for provider neutrality (fixes #549)

Rename the CAPZ_USER environment variable and related Go identifiers to
CAPI_USER, since the user prefix is not Azure-specific and applies
equally to ROSA/CAPA deployments.

Changes:
- Rename env var CAPZ_USER → CAPI_USER (with backward-compatible fallback)
- Rename Go constant DefaultCAPZUser → DefaultCAPIUser
- Rename Go struct field CAPZUser → CAPIUser
- Add getCAPIUser() helper: prefers CAPI_USER, falls back to CAPZ_USER
- Update Makefile: CAPI_USER with CAPZ_USER fallback via ifdef
- Update cleanup script: CAPI_USER with CAPZ_USER fallback
- Update all documentation (30 files)

Backward compatibility:
- Setting CAPZ_USER still works in Go code, Makefile, and scripts
- No breaking changes for existing CI/CD pipelines or user configs

Fixes #549

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: remove CAPZ_USER backward compatibility

Remove all CAPZ_USER fallback logic — the old name is no longer
supported. CAPI_USER is now the only accepted env var name.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: remove transition banner and update scope descriptions

Remove the outdated "formerly ARO-CAPZ Test Suite" transition banner
and update ARO-only scope descriptions to reflect the current
multi-provider (ARO + ROSA) support.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(camera): fix OOM crash at the source - camera capture and gallery

ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS:
The crash happens BEFORE CropImageActivity, in CameraFragment:
1. imageProxyToBitmap() decoded full-size image (4000x3000, ~46MB)
2. Bitmap.createBitmap() for rotation created ANOTHER copy (92MB total!)
3. uriToBitmap() loaded full-size gallery images
4. saveBitmapToTempFile() saved massive files
5. No bitmap.recycle() after save = memory leak

COMPREHENSIVE FIX:

1. imageProxyToBitmap() - Sample decode camera images
   - Added inSampleSize calculation before decode
   - Recycle original bitmap after rotation
   - Memory: 46MB → 11MB (75% reduction)

2. uriToBitmap() - Sample load gallery images
   - Android P+: Use ImageDecoder.setTargetSize()
   - Android 8: Use BitmapFactory.Options with inSampleSize
   - Max size: 2048x2048

3. cropImageResult - Sample load cropped image
   - Replace direct BitmapFactory.decodeFile()
   - Use decodeSampledBitmapFromFile()

4. takePhoto() - Recycle bitmap after save
   - Added try-finally block
   - Call bitmap.recycle() after saveBitmapToTempFile()

5. pickImage() - Recycle bitmap after save
   - Added try-finally block
   - Call bitmap.recycle() after saveBitmapToTempFile()

6. Added helper methods
   - decodeSampledBitmapFromFile()
   - calculateInSampleSize()

MEMORY IMPROVEMENTS:
- Camera capture: 92MB → 11MB (88% reduction)
- Gallery select: 46MB → 11MB (76% reduction)
- No memory leaks from unreleased bitmaps
- Temp files also smaller (faster crop loading)

TEST SCENARIOS:
- ✓ Take photo with rear camera (4000x3000)
- ✓ Select large image from gallery (5000x4000)
- ✓ Multiple captures in a row
- ✓ Switch between camera and gallery

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Phase-based TTS pipeline for LONG stories with age-specific guidelines

Implements 3-phase audio generation for LONG bedtime stories following
the long-story-generation-guidelines spec:

Text generation (prompts.py + generate_content_matrix.py):
- Fully age-specific Phase 1, 2, and 3 text prompts for all 3 age groups
- Phase 1 timing: 30-35% for 2-5/6-8, 25-30% for 9-12
- Phase 2: 2-5 uses repetition; 6-8/9-12 use longer clauses (no repetition)
- Phase 3: 2-5 incantatory; 6-8 poetic; 9-12 literary prose poetry
- Narrative content quality instructions from spec (emotional tones, text
  techniques, story resolution in Phase 2, contemplative Phase 3)
- WORD_COUNTS ~2x increase for LONG stories to hit duration targets
- Per-phase word count budgets in prompt to help LLM hit targets
- max_tokens raised to 16384 for longer generation; 5 retries for LONG

Audio generation (generate_audio.py):
- Age-specific PHASE_TTS_PARAMS (9-12 has different exag/cfg in Phase 2)
- Age-specific PHASE_VOLUME_DB (9-12: 0/-2/-5 dB vs 2-5/6-8: 0/-3/-6 dB)
- Age-specific PHASE_PARA_PAUSE_MS (Phase 3: 3.5s/3s/4.5s by age group)
- Speed values aligned to spec: P2=0.95, P3=0.88-0.90
- 1.5s silence padding between phases before crossfade
- 5s fade-out at end (spec: "5-second fade-out of narration")
- ASMR voice for Phase 3 whispered delivery
- Backward compatible: non-phased stories use legacy flow unchanged

Test script (test_long_story.py):
- End-to-end test for text + audio generation per age group

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: reduce memory usage (#203)

Signed-off-by: Takahisa.Ishikawa <takahisa.ishikawa@tier4.jp>

* fix: release Plot/figure/DataFrame objects early to reduce memory usage

Add del + gc.collect() for intermediate CARET Plot objects, Bokeh
figures, and DataFrames in analyze_node, analyze_path, analyze_topic,
and find_valid_duration to prevent unnecessary memory retention.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add gc.collect() at end of node/topic component loops

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: reduce memory usage by running add_path_to_architecture in subprocess

Architecture('lttng', trace_data) creates ~20GB of internal cache from
trace data parsing that cannot be freed by gc.collect(). By running
add_path_to_architecture in a subprocess and loading the result from
yaml, peak memory is reduced from 100+GB to ~13GB.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Takahisa.Ishikawa <takahisa.ishikawa@tier4.jp>

* Revert "fix: add gc.collect() at end of node/topic component loops" and
"fix: release Plot/figure/DataFrame objects early to reduce memory usage"

These gc.collect() and del optimizations are ineffective now that
Architecture('yaml') is used instead of Architecture('lttng').
Memory stays flat at 9.10GB through all processing phases.

This reverts commits c1cc715 and c3c96bf.

commit.message:
Limit scope on CNAO operand SCCs to CNAO namespace (#2602)

* components/linux-bridge: Scope SCC usage to operand namespace

OpenShift SCC admission orders applicable SCCs by priority and evaluates
them in that order (highest priority first); the SCC selected for a pod
can therefore be influenced by SCC priority and by how broadly “use” is
granted [0].
Granting SCC access via the SCC object `users/groups` is cluster-scoped,
so it can make the SCC applicable outside the CNAO operand namespace
when identities match a cluster-wide grant.
Move the linux-bridge SCC grant to RBAC (`use` on
`securitycontextconstraints/<scc-name>`) and bind it with a namespaced
RoleBinding in the operand namespace, preventing the SCC from being
chosen cluster-wide while keeping any required cluster-wide API
permissions separate.

[0]
https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/openshift_container_platform/4.20/html/authentication_and_authorization/managing-pod-security-policies#scc-prioritization_configuring-internal-oauth

Assisted-by: Claude Upus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Ram Lavi <ralavi@redhat.com>

* components/bridge-marker: Scope SCC usage to operand namespace

OpenShift SCC admission orders applicable SCCs by priority and evaluates
them in that order (highest priority first); the SCC selected for a pod
can therefore be influenced by SCC priority and by how broadly “use” is
granted [0].
Granting SCC access via the SCC object `users/groups` is cluster-scoped,
so it can make the SCC applicable outside the CNAO operand namespace
when identities match a cluster-wide grant.
Move the bridge-marker SCC grant to RBAC (`use` on
`securitycontextconstraints/<scc-name>`) and bind it with a namespaced
RoleBinding in the operand namespace, preventing the SCC from being
chosen cluster-wide while keeping any required cluster-wide API
permissions separate.

[0]
https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/openshift_container_platform/4.20/html/authentication_and_authorization/managing-pod-security-policies#scc-prioritization_configuring-internal-oauth

Assisted-by: Claude Upus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Ram Lavi <ralavi@redhat.com>

commit.message: fix(home): make partner carousel logos large and readable - Logo height: h-20 (80px) mobile, h-24 (96px) desktop - Logo width allocation: 320px per logo (was 120px) - Remove all constraining wrappers (h-10, w-[120px], maxHeight: 40px) - SVGs use native to avoid Next.js sizing constraints - Increase gap between logos (gap-16, px-8) - Opacity bumped to 0.95 so names are legible at a glance Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6

fix(media): move flaresolverr into prowlarr pod as sidecar

flaresolverr and prowlarr need to share the same VPN IP for
CloudFlare bypass cookies to work

commit.message:
Add Phase 8: Calm timer session — timestamp-based engine, state machine, soft completion

Timestamp-driven session engine with explicit state machine
(idle/running/paused/completed_active/ended). Wall-clock timer keeps
running through background/lock. Minutes-only display with step-based
SVG progress ring. Soft completion: 80% early-complete threshold
(12/24/48 min for 15/30/60 min rituals). Overtime display (+M:SS),
one-time completion haptic, back-button confirmation with threshold logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
